name: same weight re-expressed lb->kg at the same instant raises a false fact_conflict
description: Adversarial unit-change probe. Note 1 records Me.bodyWeight = 180 lb for a given instant; note 2 records the SAME reading at the SAME validity instant re-expressed as 81.6 kg (180 lb ~= 81.6 kg — physically the same measurement, just unit-converted). supersession.values_equal compares value_json dicts literally, so {value:180,unit:lb} != {value:81.6,unit:kg}; with kind=measurement and identical valid_from this hits the same-instant clash branch and files a fact_conflict, parking the second reading in pending_review. The two are the SAME measurement, so flagging them as disagreeing is a false positive — a model that normalizes units between captures poisons the time-series with spurious conflicts. SPEC-CORRECT expectation encoded: no fact_conflict, one active reading. XFAIL: values_equal has no unit-normalization, so a false fact_conflict fires. Failing line: "expected NO review item ... fact_conflict ... found 1" (and the second reading is pending_review, not silently merged).
xfail: Unit-change false conflict: supersession.values_equal compares value_json verbatim with no unit normalization, so the same measurement re-expressed in a different unit at the same instant collides as a fact_conflict instead of being recognized as identical (docs/ANALYSIS.md measurement row: 'same metric+time disagreeing -> review' fires even when they don't actually disagree).

steps.body: Weighed in at 180 lb this morning.
Correcting yesterday's weigh-in to metric: 81.6 kg.
